title: You are a senior UI/UX engineer and responsive frontend architect.
content:
[Tue, May 19, 2026 at 10:08 AM]

You are a senior UI/UX engineer and responsive frontend architect.

Your task is to transform this application into a fully responsive, production-quality multi-device platform that works smoothly on:

1. iPhone
2. Android phones
3. Samsung Z Fold outer display
4. Samsung Z Fold inner display
5. iPad portrait
6. iPad landscape
7. Tablets
8. Laptops
9. Desktop monitors

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The app must feel:
- professional
- fast
- modern
- touch-friendly
- visually clean
- stable on all screen sizes

Do NOT remove existing functionality.

ONLY improve:
- responsiveness
- layout adaptation
- usability
- spacing
- navigation
- scrolling
- touch interaction
- mobile workflow
- tablet workflow
- desktop workflow

The app currently contains:
- To-Do cards
- Lesson generator
- Rubric workflows
- PDF previews
- Upload panels
- Assignment generation
- Marking workflows
- Status banners
- Progress tracking
- Modal dialogs
- Tables
- Action buttons
- Sidebars
- Multi-stage workflows

All of these must become responsive.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CORE RESPONSIVE STRATEGY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use MOBILE-FIRST design.

Design hierarchy:
1. Mobile
2. Foldables
3. Tablet
4. Laptop/Desktop

Use responsive CSS, flexbox, and CSS grid.

Prevent ALL horizontal scrolling.

Ensure smooth resizing and orientation changes.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
BREAKPOINTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use these breakpoints:

Mobile:
0px — 699px

Tablet/Fold Inner:
700px — 1023px

Laptop/Desktop:
1024px+

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DEVICE-SPECIFIC BEHAVIOUR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

========================
1. IPHONE + SMALL MOBILE
========================

Layout:
- Single-column layout
- Vertical stacking
- Full-width cards
- Compact spacing
- Touch-first design

Requirements:
- Hide desktop sidebars
- Use hamburger menu
- Use bottom navigation bar
- Use large touch targets
- Minimum button height: 44px
- Full-width action buttons where appropriate
- Prevent clipped content
- Prevent overflow
- Prevent horizontal scrolling
- Use collapsible sections
- Convert dense tables into card layouts
- Allow swipe-friendly interaction
- Ensure forms are easy to use

Modals:
- Full-screen modal on mobile
- Sticky close button
- Scrollable content

To-Do Cards:
- Stack vertically
- Show only critical information
- Move secondary actions into:
  ⋯ overflow menu

Workflow Panels:
- Collapse advanced controls
- Use expandable accordions

PDF Preview:
- Full-screen viewer on mobile
- Zoom support
- Responsive scaling

========================
2. SAMSUNG Z FOLD OUTER
========================

Treat like compact mobile.

Requirements:
- Very clean spacing
- Compact cards
- No overcrowding
- Large readable text
- Simplified actions
- Use overflow menus
- Keep layouts narrow and efficient

========================
3. SAMSUNG Z FOLD INNER
========================

Treat as tablet.

Requirements:
- Two-column layout
- Navigation on left
- Main workspace on right
- Side-by-side workflow panels where possible
- Allow draggable panels if supported
- Improve multitasking usability

========================
4. IPAD PORTRAIT
========================

Layout:
- Tablet mode
- Two-column layout

Requirements:
- Sidebar visible
- Main workspace optimized for touch
- Larger cards
- Better spacing
- Floating preview panels
- Large modal dialogs
- Preserve readability

========================
5. IPAD LANDSCAPE
========================

Layout:
- Expanded tablet layout

Requirements:
- Optional three-panel layout
- Left navigation
- Main workspace
- Right preview/status panel

Support:
- drag panels
- split-screen feel
- side-by-side workflows

========================
6. LAPTOP + DESKTOP
========================

Layout:
- Full productivity dashboard

Use:
LEFT:
- navigation
- courses
- filters
- lesson libraries

CENTER:
- lesson generation
- marking
- uploads
- workflows

RIGHT:
- preview
- rubric
- progress logs
- QC reports
- status

Requirements:
- efficient use of space
- fixed/sticky sidebars
- resize-friendly panels
- professional dashboard feel

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
LAYOUT REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use:
- CSS Grid
- Flexbox
- Responsive containers

Requirements:
- consistent spacing
- responsive typography
- responsive padding
- responsive margins
- responsive gaps

Cards:
- rounded corners
- soft shadows
- mobile spacing
- adaptive height

Tables:
- responsive tables
- horizontal scroll ONLY inside tables if unavoidable
- mobile card conversion for narrow screens

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TYPOGRAPHY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Requirements:
- readable on all devices
- no tiny text
- proper line spacing
- scalable typography

Use responsive font scaling.

Ensure:
- headings remain readable
- buttons remain readable
- forms remain readable

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
NAVIGATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Mobile:
- bottom navigation
- hamburger menu
- slide-out drawer

Tablet:
- collapsible sidebar

Desktop:
- fixed sidebar

Ensure navigation:
- remains accessible
- does not overlap content
- works smoothly on touch devices

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TOUCH OPTIMIZATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Requirements:
- touch-friendly controls
- minimum 44px tap targets
- proper spacing between buttons
- prevent accidental taps

Support:
- swipe gestures where appropriate
- smooth scrolling
- mobile momentum scrolling

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MODAL REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Mobile:
- full-screen modals

Tablet:
- large centered dialogs

Desktop:
- centered modals with max width

Requirements:
- scrollable content
- sticky close button
- prevent modal overflow
- responsive forms

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
WORKFLOW UI REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The following workflows MUST remain easy to use on all devices:

1. Lesson generation
2. Assignment generation
3. Rubric generation
4. Marking workflows
5. QC workflows
6. Uploading scripts
7. Viewing PDFs
8. Viewing reports
9. Progress tracking
10. Stage/status banners

Status banners must:
- wrap correctly
- remain readable
- adapt to small screens

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Optimize for:
- fast loading
- smooth scrolling
- minimal layout shifting
- responsive rendering

Use:
- lazy loading where appropriate
- responsive image loading
- optimized rendering

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
RESPONSIVE TESTING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Test and fix layouts for:

1. iPhone portrait
2. iPhone landscape
3. Android portrait
4. Android landscape
5. Samsung Z Fold outer display
6. Samsung Z Fold inner display
7. iPad portrait
8. iPad landscape
9. Small laptop
10. Large desktop monitor

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FIX ALL OF THE FOLLOWING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

- overlapping panels
- clipped buttons
- hidden content
- oversized tables
- unreadable text
- overflowing modals
- broken flex layouts
- broken grids
- sidebar overlap
- viewport overflow
- inconsistent spacing
- unusable mobile forms
- tiny touch targets
- layout jumping
- scaling issues

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL GOAL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The final application should feel like:
- a premium SaaS platform
- modern educational software
- enterprise workflow software
- optimized for both desktop productivity and mobile usability

The app must feel natural and polished on:
- phone
- foldable
- tablet
- laptop
- desktop

Maintain all existing features while dramatically improving responsiveness and usability.

You are a senior UI/UX engineer and responsive frontend architect.

Your task is to transform this application into a fully responsive, production-quality multi-device platform that works smoothly on:

1. iPhone
2. Android phones
3. Samsung Z Fold outer display
4. Samsung Z Fold inner display
5. iPad portrait
6. iPad landscape
7. Tablets
8. Laptops
9. Desktop monitors

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The app must feel:
- professional
- fast
- modern
- touch-friendly
- visually clean
- stable on all screen sizes

Do NOT remove existing functionality.

ONLY improve:
- responsiveness
- layout adaptation
- usability
- spacing
- navigation
- scrolling
- touch interaction
- mobile workflow
- tablet workflow
- desktop workflow

The app currently contains:
- To-Do cards
- Lesson generator
- Rubric workflows
- PDF previews
- Upload panels
- Assignment generation
- Marking workflows
- Status banners
- Progress tracking
- Modal dialogs
- Tables
- Action buttons
- Sidebars
- Multi-stage workflows

All of these must become responsive.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CORE RESPONSIVE STRATEGY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use MOBILE-FIRST design.

Design hierarchy:
1. Mobile
2. Foldables
3. Tablet
4. Laptop/Desktop

Use responsive CSS, flexbox, and CSS grid.

Prevent ALL horizontal scrolling.

Ensure smooth resizing and orientation changes.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
BREAKPOINTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use these breakpoints:

Mobile:
0px — 699px

Tablet/Fold Inner:
700px — 1023px

Laptop/Desktop:
1024px+

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DEVICE-SPECIFIC BEHAVIOUR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

========================
1. IPHONE + SMALL MOBILE
========================

Layout:
- Single-column layout
- Vertical stacking
- Full-width cards
- Compact spacing
- Touch-first design

Requirements:
- Hide desktop sidebars
- Use hamburger menu
- Use bottom navigation bar
- Use large touch targets
- Minimum button height: 44px
- Full-width action buttons where appropriate
- Prevent clipped content
- Prevent overflow
- Prevent horizontal scrolling
- Use collapsible sections
- Convert dense tables into card layouts
- Allow swipe-friendly interaction
- Ensure forms are easy to use

Modals:
- Full-screen modal on mobile
- Sticky close button
- Scrollable content

To-Do Cards:
- Stack vertically
- Show only critical information
- Move secondary actions into:
  ⋯ overflow menu

Workflow Panels:
- Collapse advanced controls
- Use expandable accordions

PDF Preview:
- Full-screen viewer on mobile
- Zoom support
- Responsive scaling

========================
2. SAMSUNG Z FOLD OUTER
========================

Treat like compact mobile.

Requirements:
- Very clean spacing
- Compact cards
- No overcrowding
- Large readable text
- Simplified actions
- Use overflow menus
- Keep layouts narrow and efficient

========================
3. SAMSUNG Z FOLD INNER
========================

Treat as tablet.

Requirements:
- Two-column layout
- Navigation on left
- Main workspace on right
- Side-by-side workflow panels where possible
- Allow draggable panels if supported
- Improve multitasking usability

========================
4. IPAD PORTRAIT
========================

Layout:
- Tablet mode
- Two-column layout

Requirements:
- Sidebar visible
- Main workspace optimized for touch
- Larger cards
- Better spacing
- Floating preview panels
- Large modal dialogs
- Preserve readability

========================
5. IPAD LANDSCAPE
========================

Layout:
- Expanded tablet layout

Requirements:
- Optional three-panel layout
- Left navigation
- Main workspace
- Right preview/status panel

Support:
- drag panels
- split-screen feel
- side-by-side workflows

========================
6. LAPTOP + DESKTOP
========================

Layout:
- Full productivity dashboard

Use:
LEFT:
- navigation
- courses
- filters
- lesson libraries

CENTER:
- lesson generation
- marking
- uploads
- workflows

RIGHT:
- preview
- rubric
- progress logs
- QC reports
- status

Requirements:
- efficient use of space
- fixed/sticky sidebars
- resize-friendly panels
- professional dashboard feel

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
LAYOUT REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use:
- CSS Grid
- Flexbox
- Responsive containers

Requirements:
- consistent spacing
- responsive typography
- responsive padding
- responsive margins
- responsive gaps

Cards:
- rounded corners
- soft shadows
- mobile spacing
- adaptive height

Tables:
- responsive tables
- horizontal scroll ONLY inside tables if unavoidable
- mobile card conversion for narrow screens

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TYPOGRAPHY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Requirements:
- readable on all devices
- no tiny text
- proper line spacing
- scalable typography

Use responsive font scaling.

Ensure:
- headings remain readable
- buttons remain readable
- forms remain readable

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
NAVIGATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Mobile:
- bottom navigation
- hamburger menu
- slide-out drawer

Tablet:
- collapsible sidebar

Desktop:
- fixed sidebar

Ensure navigation:
- remains accessible
- does not overlap content
- works smoothly on touch devices

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TOUCH OPTIMIZATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Requirements:
- touch-friendly controls
- minimum 44px tap targets
- proper spacing between buttons
- prevent accidental taps

Support:
- swipe gestures where appropriate
- smooth scrolling
- mobile momentum scrolling

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MODAL REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Mobile:
- full-screen modals

Tablet:
- large centered dialogs

Desktop:
- centered modals with max width

Requirements:
- scrollable content
- sticky close button
- prevent modal overflow
- responsive forms

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
WORKFLOW UI REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The following workflows MUST remain easy to use on all devices:

1. Lesson generation
2. Assignment generation
3. Rubric generation
4. Marking workflows
5. QC workflows
6. Uploading scripts
7. Viewing PDFs
8. Viewing reports
9. Progress tracking
10. Stage/status banners

Status banners must:
- wrap correctly
- remain readable
- adapt to small screens

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Optimize for:
- fast loading
- smooth scrolling
- minimal layout shifting
- responsive rendering

Use:
- lazy loading where appropriate
- responsive image loading
- optimized rendering

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
RESPONSIVE TESTING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Test and fix layouts for:

1. iPhone portrait
2. iPhone landscape
3. Android portrait
4. Android landscape
5. Samsung Z Fold outer display
6. Samsung Z Fold inner display
7. iPad portrait
8. iPad landscape
9. Small laptop
10. Large desktop monitor

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FIX ALL OF THE FOLLOWING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

- overlapping panels
- clipped buttons
- hidden content
- oversized tables
- unreadable text
- overflowing modals
- broken flex layouts
- broken grids
- sidebar overlap
- viewport overflow
- inconsistent spacing
- unusable mobile forms
- tiny touch targets
- layout jumping
- scaling issues

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL GOAL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The final application should feel like:
- a premium SaaS platform
- modern educational software
- enterprise workflow software
- optimized for both desktop productivity and mobile usability

The app must feel natural and polished on:
- phone
- foldable
- tablet
- laptop
- desktop

Maintain all existing features while dramatically improving responsiveness and usability.
[Tue, May 19, 2026 at 10:08 AM]

title: You are a senior UI/UX architect and frontend systems engineer.
content:
You are a senior UI/UX architect and frontend systems engineer.

Redesign and upgrade the Notebook System into a modern, enterprise-grade, fully responsive notebook workspace optimized for:

- Laptop
- Desktop
- iPad
- Android tablets
- iPhone
- Android phones
- Samsung Z Fold outer display
- Samsung Z Fold inner display

The current notebook system forces users to move page-by-page to locate content, which is inefficient and slows down teaching, marking, lesson generation, rubric review, QC workflows, and report analysis.

The new notebook system must become:
- searchable
- fast
- scalable
- touch-friendly
- professional
- productivity-focused
- responsive
- easy to navigate

DO NOT remove existing notebook functionality.

ONLY improve:
- notebook navigation
- page management
- page discovery
- responsiveness
- usability
- workflow speed
- mobile experience
- tablet experience
- desktop productivity

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Users must be able to:

1. Instantly locate notebook pages
2. Jump directly to any page
3. Search notebook content quickly
4. Navigate efficiently on all devices
5. Delete, restore, organize, and manage pages safely
6. Avoid page-by-page clicking
7. Work comfortably during marking, lesson generation, QC, rubric review, and PDF analysis

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CORE NOTEBOOK TOOLBAR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Create a professional notebook toolbar containing:

1. Page Dropdown Selector
2. Notebook Search
3. Previous Page Button
4. Next Page Button
5. Page Counter
6. Add Page Button
7. Page Actions Menu
8. Optional Bookmarks/Favorites
9. Optional Recently Viewed Pages
10. Optional Sidebar Index Toggle

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. PAGE DROPDOWN SELECTOR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add a dropdown menu listing ALL notebook pages.

Requirements:
- instantly selectable
- searchable
- scrollable
- touch-friendly
- responsive

Each page entry should display:
- page number
- FULL page title
- optional category/type
- optional icon

Examples:

Page 1 — Course Overview
Page 2 — Week 1 Lesson 1
Page 3 — Assignment Brief
Page 4 — Rubric
Page 5 — Student Feedback
Page 6 — QC Notes

Long title examples:

Week 3 Lesson 2 — Area Between Two Curves Using Integration
ENGR430 — ATMega328P Interrupt Programming and Timer Configuration
Student Feedback — Communication Systems Final Examination

If no title exists:
- fallback to Page 1, Page 2, etc.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FULL TITLE DISPLAY REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The dropdown MUST display full notebook page titles.

Do NOT unnecessarily truncate titles.

Avoid:
- ...
- clipped text
- hidden text
- narrow dropdowns

Requirements:

1. Automatically resize dropdown width intelligently.

2. On desktop and tablet:
   - allow wide dropdown menus
   - allow multi-line wrapped titles
   - preserve readability

3. On mobile:
   - allow wrapped text
   - increase item height automatically
   - prevent horizontal scrolling
   - preserve touch usability

4. Keep the currently selected page title fully visible in the notebook toolbar.

5. Add tooltip support on desktop for extremely long titles.

6. Ensure dropdown items remain:
   - touch-friendly
   - easy to scan
   - visually clean

7. Preserve performance even with:
   - many pages
   - long notebook titles

The selected page must:
- become highlighted
- automatically load
- scroll into view if needed

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. SEARCHABLE NOTEBOOK FINDER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add intelligent notebook search.

Users should be able to search by:
- page title
- keywords
- lesson name
- student name
- rubric
- topic
- notes
- assignment title

The search should:
- filter notebook pages live
- instantly narrow dropdown results
- support partial matches
- remain fast even with many pages

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. QUICK PAGE JUMPING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Users should be able to:
- instantly jump to any page
- avoid repeated Next/Previous clicking

Requirements:
- immediate page switching
- smooth transitions
- preserve scroll state if possible

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. PREVIOUS/NEXT BUTTONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Keep Previous and Next buttons.

However:
- they become secondary navigation
- dropdown/search becomes primary navigation

Buttons must:
- remain touch-friendly
- support keyboard shortcuts on desktop
- support swipe gestures on mobile/tablet

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. PAGE COUNTER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Display:
- current page
- total pages

Example:
Page 5 of 32

Keep visible at all times.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. PAGE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add professional page management features.

Each notebook page must support:
- rename
- duplicate
- move
- delete
- restore
- bookmark/star
- optional lock/protect

Use a professional three-dot menu:
⋯

Examples:
- Rename Page
- Duplicate Page
- Move Page
- Delete Page
- Add to Favorites
- Lock Page

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. DELETE + TRASH SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

DO NOT permanently delete pages immediately.

Instead:

Delete → Move to Trash → Restore or Permanently Delete

Requirements:

1. Add a Trash/Recycle Bin system.

2. Deleted pages should move into Trash.

3. Users should be able to:
   - restore deleted pages
   - permanently delete pages
   - empty trash

4. Before deleting:
   - show confirmation dialog

Example:
"Are you sure you want to delete this notebook page?"

5. Add Undo support.

Example:
"Page deleted — Undo"

6. Allow bulk delete for multiple selected pages.

7. Prevent accidental deletion on touch devices.

8. Protected pages should require additional confirmation before deletion.

9. Deleted pages must NOT break:
   - notebook numbering
   - references
   - workflows
   - lesson generation
   - marking workflows
   - rubric workflows
   - QC systems
   - generated reports

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. NOTEBOOK SIDEBAR INDEX
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add an optional collapsible notebook sidebar.

The sidebar should:
- list all notebook pages
- allow quick jumping
- highlight active page
- support categories/sections
- support nested page groups

Example:

▼ Week 1
   • Lesson 1
   • Lesson 2

▼ Assignments
   • Rubric
   • QC Notes

▼ Student Scripts
   • Student A
   • Student B

Requirements:
- collapsible
- searchable
- responsive
- smooth scrolling

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. BOOKMARKS / FAVORITES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Allow users to:
- star important pages
- pin commonly used pages
- quickly reopen critical notebook sections

Examples:
⭐ Rubric
⭐ Final Marks
⭐ QC Summary

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. RECENTLY VIEWED PAGES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Optionally track:
- recently opened notebook pages

Allow quick reopening.

Example:
Recent:
- Student Feedback
- Assignment Rubric
- Week 4 Notes

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11. RESPONSIVE DESIGN
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

========================
MOBILE (iPhone / Android)
========================

Requirements:
- full-width dropdown
- large touch targets
- large readable text
- compact toolbar
- sticky notebook toolbar
- prevent horizontal scrolling

Use:
- stacked toolbar layout
- collapsible advanced controls

Navigation must feel:
- fast
- touch-friendly
- uncluttered

========================
SAMSUNG Z FOLD OUTER
========================

Treat as compact mobile.

Requirements:
- simplified toolbar
- large controls
- compact spacing
- overflow menus for secondary actions

========================
TABLET / Z FOLD INNER / IPAD
========================

Requirements:
- horizontal toolbar
- larger dropdown
- side-by-side controls
- efficient use of width
- improved multitasking feel

========================
LAPTOP / DESKTOP
========================

Requirements:
- professional notebook toolbar
- keyboard shortcuts
- hover states
- quick-access actions
- optimized productivity workflow

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
12. MODAL + DIALOG SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Mobile:
- full-screen dialogs

Tablet:
- large centered dialogs

Desktop:
- professional centered modal windows

Requirements:
- scrollable content
- sticky action buttons
- responsive forms
- no clipped content

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Optimize for:
- large notebooks
- many pages
- rapid switching
- mobile responsiveness

Requirements:
- smooth scrolling
- fast rendering
- lazy loading where appropriate
- minimal layout shifting

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
14. ACCESSIBILITY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Requirements:
- keyboard navigation
- visible focus states
- touch accessibility
- screen-reader-friendly labels
- large tap targets
- readable typography

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
15. UI/UX QUALITY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The final notebook system should feel like:
- Notion
- OneNote
- Obsidian
- premium educational software
- enterprise SaaS productivity software

Requirements:
- clean spacing
- modern styling
- polished transitions
- intuitive interaction
- responsive layouts
- professional typography

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
16. FINAL GOAL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The notebook system should become:
- fast
- searchable
- scalable
- responsive
- touch-friendly
- professional
- enterprise-grade

Users must be able to:
- instantly find notebook pages
- jump directly to content
- search efficiently
- safely delete and restore pages
- organize notebooks professionally
- work comfortably on all devices
- navigate large notebooks effortlessly

Do not remove existing notebook functionality.

Only upgrade the notebook navigation, notebook management, responsiveness, and usability experience to a modern professional standard.

Users should be able to search by:
- page title
- keywords
- lesson name
- student name
- rubric
- topic
- notes
- assignment title

The search should:
- filter notebook pages live
- instantly narrow dropdown results
- support partial matches
- remain fast even with many pages

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. QUICK PAGE JUMPING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Users should be able to:
- instantly jump to any page
- avoid repeated Next/Previous clicking

Requirements:
- immediate page switching
- smooth transitions
- preserve scroll state if possible

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. PREVIOUS/NEXT BUTTONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Keep Previous and Next buttons.

However:
- they become secondary navigation
- dropdown/search becomes primary navigation

Buttons must:
- remain touch-friendly
- support keyboard shortcuts on desktop
- support swipe gestures on mobile/tablet

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. PAGE COUNTER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Display:
- current page
- total pages

Example:
Page 5 of 32

Keep visible at all times.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. PAGE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add professional page management features.

Each notebook page must support:
- rename
- duplicate
- move
- delete
- restore
- bookmark/star
- optional lock/protect

Use a professional three-dot menu:
⋯

Examples:
- Rename Page
- Duplicate Page
- Move Page
- Delete Page
- Add to Favorites
- Lock Page

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. DELETE + TRASH SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

DO NOT permanently delete pages immediately.

Instead:

Delete → Move to Trash → Restore or Permanently Delete

Requirements:

1. Add a Trash/Recycle Bin system.

2. Deleted pages should move into Trash.

3. Users should be able to:
   - restore deleted pages
   - permanently delete pages
   - empty trash

4. Before deleting:
   - show confirmation dialog

Example:
"Are you sure you want to delete this notebook page?"

5. Add Undo support.

Example:
"Page deleted — Undo"

6. Allow bulk delete for multiple selected pages.

7. Prevent accidental deletion on touch devices.

8. Protected pages should require additional confirmation before deletion.

9. Deleted pages must NOT break:
   - notebook numbering
   - references
   - workflows
   - lesson generation
   - marking workflows
   - rubric workflows
   - QC systems
   - generated reports

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. NOTEBOOK SIDEBAR INDEX
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add an optional collapsible notebook sidebar.

The sidebar should:
- list all notebook pages
- allow quick jumping
- highlight active page
- support categories/sections
- support nested page groups

Example:

▼ Week 1
   • Lesson 1
   • Lesson 2

▼ Assignments
   • Rubric
   • QC Notes

▼ Student Scripts
   • Student A
   • Student B

Requirements:
- collapsible
- searchable
- responsive
- smooth scrolling

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. BOOKMARKS / FAVORITES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Allow users to:
- star important pages
- pin commonly used pages
- quickly reopen critical notebook sections

Examples:
⭐ Rubric
⭐ Final Marks
⭐ QC Summary

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. RECENTLY VIEWED PAGES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Optionally track:
- recently opened notebook pages

Allow quick reopening.

Example:
Recent:
- Student Feedback
- Assignment Rubric
- Week 4 Notes

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

title: Main Workflows
content:
[Tue, May 19, 2026, 08:48 AM]

Main Workflows
Generate a Lesson
Press N (keyboard shortcut) or click Lessons → Manage → Generate
Pick a course, week, lesson number, topic
Click Generate — the AI writes a full YAML lesson plan
Schedule
Lessons → Schedule Engine to upload or build a course schedule (CSV)
Once a schedule exists, open it and click "Export to To-Do" to push every lesson into your To-Do List as action cards
To-Do List
Open via sidebar To-Do or Lessons → Manage
Cards have course code, week/lesson, due date, and quick action buttons
Click the classroom icon on any card starting with a course code to send it to Google Classroom
Marking & Student Scripts
Upload student PDFs in Resource Library → Student Scripts
AI marks them automatically
Feedback PDFs generated per student
Research Workspace
Knowledge Base → Research — paste URLs, PDFs, YouTube links
AI extracts and summarises content to inform lesson generation
Lesson Files (PDF/LaTeX)
Open a lesson → Lesson Files (or press Cmd/Ctrl+T)
Generate PDF, compile LaTeX, export to Overleaf, push to GitHub
Keyboard Shortcuts
Key	Action
N	New lesson
P	Pipeline mode
M	Manage lessons
S	AI Settings
?	Show all shortcuts
Cmd+Y	Open YAML for selected lesson
Cmd+T	Open lesson files
Mobile
Bottom bar: Home / Lessons / Courses / To-Do / Status
Hamburger menu (top-left): all nav menus
All modals open full-screen on phone

content:
[Wed, May 20, 2026 at 12:30 PM]

Yes — that is actually a very good feature for your app.

You can allow students to email assignments, scripts, or questions directly into your app’s To-Do List system automatically.

Recommended Workflow

Student Sends Email

Student emails:

* Assignment script
* PDF/photo/document
* Subject line

Example:

Subject:
MATH095 - Ratios Assignment - John Brown

Attachment:

* PDF
* JPG
* DOCX

⸻

Your App Automatically Does This

1. Reads Incoming Email

Using:

* Gmail API￼
    or
* Microsoft Graph API￼

⸻

2. Extracts Information

Your app extracts:

Item	Example
Student Name	John Brown
Course	MATH095
Topic	Ratios
Attachment	Uploaded PDF
Timestamp	Auto-generated

⸻

3. Creates To-Do Card Automatically

Example card:

📌 MATH095 – Ratios Assignment
👤 John Brown
📄 Script Uploaded
📅 May 20, 2026
📥 Source: Email
🟡 Awaiting Marking

⸻

BEST Architecture for Your App

Option 1 — Gmail + n8n (Recommended)

This is the easiest and most scalable.

Flow

Student Email
      ↓
Gmail Inbox
      ↓
n8n watches inbox
      ↓
Downloads attachment
      ↓
Renames file
      ↓
Uploads to Supabase
      ↓
Creates To-Do card
      ↓
Starts marking workflow

⸻

What You Need

Tool	Purpose
n8n￼	Automation
Gmail API￼	Read emails
Supabase￼	Store files/data
Bolt.new￼	Frontend
Gemini API	OCR + marking
ChatGPT API	QC

⸻

Suggested Email Format

Tell students:

Subject Format

COURSECODE - TOPIC - STUDENT NAME

Example:

ENGR405 - Modulation Assignment - Sarah Lewis

⸻

Automatic File Naming

Your app can rename uploads automatically:

MATH095_Ratios_JohnBrown_Script.pdf

Assignment paper:

MATH095_Ratios_Assignment.pdf

Rubric:

MATH095_Ratios_Rubric.pdf

⸻

Advanced Features You Can Add

Auto Student Matching

Use student email address to:

* identify student
* connect to course
* track submissions

⸻

Duplicate Detection

Prevent students from uploading same script twice.

⸻

Auto Reply Email

Your app can reply:

Dear Student,

Your assignment submission has been received successfully.

Course: MATH095
Topic: Ratios Assignment

Your script is now awaiting marking.

Thank you.

⸻

Smart To-Do Columns

Status	Meaning
Uploaded	Email received
OCR Running	Reading script
Marking	AI marking
QC Review	ChatGPT audit
Complete	Final PDF ready

⸻

BEST Setup for You

Based on your app architecture, I would strongly recommend:

Phase 1

Build:

* Gmail watcher
* Email parser
* Attachment uploader
* Auto to-do card creator

This alone will save massive time.

⸻

Phase 2

Add:

* AI OCR
* Rubric matching
* Auto marking
* QC audit
* Classroom return

⸻

Bolt.new Prompt Idea

Your frontend can support:

📥 Incoming Submissions
[ Student ] [ Course ] [ Topic ] [ Attachment ] [ Status ]
[Open]
[Mark]
[Generate Rubric]
[Send Feedback]

⸻

My Recommendation

For your workflow:

BEST submission methods ranked

Method	Rating
Google Classroom	⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Email → To-Do Auto Import	⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Manual Upload	⭐⭐⭐
WhatsApp Upload	⭐⭐

Email importing is excellent because:

* students already know email
* works from any device
* easy to automate
* works internationally
* integrates well with n8n

Your app is moving toward becoming a full academic workflow platform.


Do this first

Create a dedicated submission email address.

Example:

assignments@yourdomain.com

or simply:

yourapp.submissions@gmail.com

Then do these 5 steps

1. Create the Gmail account

Use this only for student submissions.

2. Decide the email subject format

Use one strict format:

COURSECODE - TOPIC - STUDENT NAME

Example:

MATH095 - Ratios Assignment - John Brown

3. Create a Gmail label

Create a label called:

Student Submissions

4. Test with one email

Send yourself one test email with:

* subject line
* one PDF attachment
* student name
* course code

5. After that, connect n8n

n8n will watch that Gmail inbox and create the To-Do card automatically.

Best first test

Send this to the new inbox:

Subject: MATH095 - Ratios Assignment - Test Student
Message:
Please find attached my assignment script.
Attachment:
TestStudent_Script.pdf

Once this works, the next step is to build the n8n workflow: Gmail → Supabase → To-Do Card.

title: mobile usability.
content:
[Wed, May 20, 2026 at 03:39 PM]

Yes — this is a major improvement for mobile usability. Your app is now behaving much more like a proper responsive web application.

Here’s what is working well from the screenshot:

⸻

✅ What You Fixed Successfully

✅ Mobile Sidebar Navigation

The slide-out sidebar is working correctly on iPhone.

This is important because:

* desktop sidebars do not fit phones
* users can now access navigation cleanly
* the UI feels like a native app

⸻

✅ Good Touch Spacing

Your menu items are:

* large enough
* properly spaced
* finger-friendly

That is critical for:

* iPhone
* iPad
* Fold devices
* Android tablets

⸻

✅ Responsive Layout

The dashboard appears to:

* resize correctly
* preserve card proportions
* avoid horizontal overflow

Huge improvement.

⸻

✅ Professional Dark Theme

Your visual design is now consistent:

* navigation
* cards
* headers
* buttons

This makes the app feel like:

* Notion
* ClickUp
* Linear
* modern SaaS platforms

⸻

⚠️ Remaining Problems I Can Still See

1. Top Navbar Is Crowded

Your header area is still too compressed on mobile.

Example:

* “Lessons”
* “Knowledge Base”
* logo
* back arrow

They are fighting for width.

Recommended Fix

On mobile:

* collapse top menu items into a single menu button
* keep ONLY:
    * logo
    * hamburger menu
    * maybe notifications/profile

Everything else goes inside drawer menus.

⸻

2. Sidebar Width Slightly Too Large

The sidebar occupies too much horizontal space on iPhone.

Recommended Width

Use:

width: min(85vw, 320px);

This feels more modern on smaller devices.

⸻

3. Dashboard Cards Too Dense

The dashboard still feels desktop-oriented.

Cards are:

* too close together
* too information-heavy

Recommended Fix

On mobile:

* use 1-column stacking
* larger vertical spacing
* hide secondary stats

⸻

VERY IMPORTANT NEXT STEP

Your app now needs:

📱 Mobile Breakpoints

You should fully standardize these:

Device	Width
Mobile	< 640px
Tablet	640–1024px
Laptop	1024–1440px
Large Desktop	1440px+

⸻

Critical Features You MUST Add Next

✅ Sticky Mobile Header

Keep:

* menu button
* page title
* quick add button

Always visible.

⸻

✅ Bottom Mobile Navigation

This is one of the biggest upgrades you can make.

Example:

Icon	Feature
Home	Dashboard
Courses	Courses
To-Do	Tasks
Generate	AI
Library	Files

This dramatically improves:

* iPhone usability
* Fold usability
* tablet experience

⸻

✅ Collapsible Widgets

Your dashboard cards should:

* expand/collapse
* remember state

Especially on phones.

⸻

✅ Mobile Typesetter View

Your PDF/typesetter pages are probably still difficult on phones.

You need:

* zoom support
* horizontal pan
* responsive PDF container
* full-screen reading mode

⸻

Your App Is Now Transitioning From:

BEFORE

“Desktop site forced onto mobile”

➜ TO:

NOW

“Actual responsive teaching platform”

That is a huge step.

⸻

What I Would Do Next (Priority Order)

Priority 1

Fix mobile top navbar.

Priority 2

Add bottom mobile navigation.

Priority 3

Improve dashboard stacking/cards.

Priority 4

Tablet optimization for iPad + Fold.

Priority 5

PWA installation support:

* Add to Home Screen
* splash screen
* offline cache
* app icon

That will make TeachBlox AI feel like a real installed app.

⸻

Overall Assessment

Area	Score
Mobile responsiveness	8/10
Visual quality	9/10
Navigation	7.5/10
Professional appearance	9/10
Phone usability	7.5/10
Tablet readiness	7/10

You are much closer now to a production-quality educational SaaS platform.

title: uploaded student assessment scripts and RETURN
content:
[Thu, May 21, 2026 at 12:55 You are an AI Assessment Marking and Rubric Completion Agent.

Your job is to analyse uploaded student assessment scripts and RETURN the final result as a COMPLETED RUBRIC instead of a long narrative report.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CORE PURPOSE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system must:

1. Read:
   • Assessment Paper
   • Rubric
   • Student Script(s)

2. Mark strictly using the rubric.

3. Produce:
   • A completed rubric
   • Marks per question/criterion
   • Brief feedback
   • Grand total
   • Percentage
   • Optional short overall comment

4. Export the result cleanly for:
   • PDF generation
   • Google Classroom return
   • To-Do List storage
   • Student feedback view

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CRITICAL MARKING RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. NEVER invent marks.
2. ONLY mark visible evidence in the student script.
3. NEVER assume missing work.
4. If handwriting or evidence is unclear, state:
   "Unclear from student script."
5. If no response exists, state:
   "No response provided."
6. The rubric is the PRIMARY marking authority.
7. Every mark awarded or lost must trace directly to:
   • rubric criteria
   • visible student work
8. Maintain strict numerical accuracy.
9. Automatically calculate totals and percentages.
10. NEVER hallucinate answers or corrections.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUIRED INPUT FILES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

INPUT 1:
Assessment Paper

INPUT 2:
Rubric / Marking Scheme

INPUT 3:
Student Script(s)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The PRIMARY output must be a COMPLETED RUBRIC TABLE.

DO NOT generate long essays unless specifically requested.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
OUTPUT STRUCTURE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Generate:

1. Student Information Block
2. Completed Rubric Table
3. Section Totals
4. Grand Total
5. Percentage
6. Short Overall Feedback

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STUDENT INFO BLOCK
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Display:

• Student Name
• Student ID (if available)
• Course
• Assignment Title
• Date Marked
• Marker: AI Assessment Agent

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COMPLETED RUBRIC TABLE FORMAT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use this structure:

| Q# | Question / Criterion | Max Marks | Marks Awarded | Evidence Found | Feedback |
|----|----------------------|------------|----------------|----------------|----------|

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FEEDBACK RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Feedback must be:

• short
• precise
• educational
• audit-friendly

Examples:

• Correct answer.
• Correct method but arithmetic error.
• Formula missing.
• Units not included.
• Diagram incomplete.
• Excellent explanation.
• No response provided.
• Unclear from student script.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MARKING STYLE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system must:

• score EVERY question individually
• display EVERY awarded mark
• calculate ALL section totals
• calculate FINAL TOTAL automatically
• calculate FINAL PERCENTAGE automatically

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL SUMMARY FORMAT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Display:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL RESULTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Section A Total: xx/xx
Section B Total: xx/xx
Section C Total: xx/xx

GRAND TOTAL: xx/xx

FINAL PERCENTAGE: xx%

Overall Feedback:
[Short professional summary]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QUALITY CONTROL CHECKS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Before finalising the rubric:

✔ Verify every question was marked
✔ Verify totals are correct
✔ Verify section weighting is correct
✔ Verify no hallucinated answers exist
✔ Verify rubric alignment
✔ Verify percentage calculations
✔ Verify all feedback is professional

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
UI DISPLAY REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The completed rubric should display as:

• clean
• compact
• printable
• mobile-friendly
• PDF-friendly
• classroom-friendly

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PREFERRED DESIGN
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The rubric output should:

• fit on approximately one page where possible
• use tables
• minimise unnecessary text
• emphasise marks and corrections
• be easy for students to understand quickly

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
OPTIONAL FEATURES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If enabled:

• colour-code:
   - correct = green
   - partial = orange
   - incorrect = red

• allow:
   - PDF export
   - Classroom return
   - To-Do List attachment
   - Overleaf export

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MULTI-STUDENT SUPPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If multiple scripts are uploaded:

• analyse each script separately
• generate one completed rubric per student
• keep totals independent
• never mix student results

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL INSTRUCTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The completed rubric IS the report.

Do not generate large narrative reports unless explicitly requested.
The rubric must be the primary feedback and grading document.

OPTIONAL FEATURES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If enabled:

• colour-code:
   - correct = green
   - partial = orange
   - incorrect = red

• allow:
   - PDF export
   - Classroom return
   - To-Do List attachment
   - Overleaf export

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MULTI-STUDENT SUPPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If multiple scripts are uploaded:

• analyse each script separately
• generate one completed rubric per student
• keep totals independent
• never mix student results

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL INSTRUCTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The completed rubric IS the report.

Do not generate large narrative reports unless explicitly requested.
The rubric must be the primary feedback and grading document.
[Thu, May 21, 2026 at 12:55 AM]

title: the first complete working pipeline.
content:

You are at the point where you should build the first complete working pipeline.

Do not try to build everything at once.

Your Immediate Goal

Build this:

Upload Student Script
        ↓
OCR Extraction
        ↓
AI Marking Using Rubric
        ↓
Completed Rubric Table
        ↓
Generate PDF
        ↓
Save to Supabase

Once THIS works reliably for ONE student, everything else becomes much easier.

⸻

Recommended Next Steps

STEP 1 — Create the Database Tables (TODAY)

In  Supabase￼ create:

column

id

raw_ocr_json

marking_json

qc_json

final_score

percentage

rubric_pdf_url

status

created_at

⸻

STEP 2 — Build the Upload UI in  Bolt.new￼

Create ONE upload page.

The page should allow:

Teacher uploads:

* Assignment Paper PDF
* Rubric PDF
* Student Script PDF

Then click:

Start Marking

⸻

STEP 3 — Save Files to Supabase Storage

Create buckets:

assignment-papers
rubrics
student-scripts
marked-reports

⸻

STEP 4 — Add OCR (MOST IMPORTANT)

Start with:

Recommended OCR

* Google Gemini API￼

Your OCR job:

Extract:
- question numbers
- student answers
- diagrams
- calculations
- visible work only

Save OCR output as JSON.

⸻

STEP 5 — Add the Marking Prompt

Use the completed rubric prompt you already created.

Input:

* OCR JSON
* Rubric
* Assessment paper

Output:

{
  "question": "Q1",
  "max_marks": 5,
  "awarded_marks": 4,
  "feedback": "Correct method but arithmetic error."
}

⸻

STEP 6 — Generate the Completed Rubric

This is your MAIN REPORT.

Display:

Q#	Max	Awarded	Feedback

Plus:

* Grand Total
* Percentage
* Overall Comment

⸻

STEP 7 — Export PDF

Generate:

ENGR405_JohnBrown_Rubric.pdf

This becomes:

* the student report
* the grading sheet
* the classroom attachment

⸻

STEP 8 — ONLY AFTER THAT

Then add:

Feature	Add Later
Google Classroom sync	Later
Email ingestion	Later
Multiple student batch marking	Later
QC agent	Later
Overleaf export	Later
Analytics dashboard	Later

⸻

Your REAL Priority

Right now your priority is NOT:

* mobile optimisation
* dashboards
* notebook systems
* advanced AI orchestration
* fancy UI

Your priority is:

ONE STUDENT
ONE RUBRIC
ONE PDF
WORKING RELIABLY

That is the foundation of the entire system.

title: Your task is to process uploaded assessment documents and prepare them for AI marking
content:
[Thu, May 21, 2026 at 12:59 AM]

You are an AI Assessment Processing Agent.

Your task is to process uploaded assessment documents and prepare them for AI marking.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system must:

1. Receive uploaded files
2. Validate the files
3. Organise the files
4. Extract OCR text
5. Identify questions and answers
6. Prepare structured JSON for marking
7. Send clean structured data to the marking agent

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
EXPECTED INPUT FILES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system expects THREE uploaded files:

1. Assessment Paper
2. Rubric / Marking Scheme
3. Student Script

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CRITICAL RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. NEVER hallucinate missing content.
2. ONLY extract visible information.
3. If text is unreadable, state:
   "Unreadable from script."
4. If a page is blank, state:
   "Blank page."
5. Preserve question numbering exactly.
6. Preserve mathematical notation exactly where possible.
7. Preserve tables, diagrams, labels and units.
8. Maintain page order.
9. Separate student answers by question.
10. Do NOT mark the work at this stage.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 1 — FILE VALIDATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Verify:

✔ Assessment paper uploaded
✔ Rubric uploaded
✔ Student script uploaded

If any file is missing:

Return:
"MISSING REQUIRED FILE"

Specify which file is missing.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 2 — FILE CLASSIFICATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Classify uploaded files as:

• assessment_paper
• rubric
• student_script

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 3 — OCR EXTRACTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Extract:

• typed text
• handwritten text
• equations
• calculations
• diagrams
• labels
• tables
• units
• graph annotations

DO NOT interpret meaning yet.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 4 — QUESTION DETECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Identify:

• question numbers
• subparts
• sections
• page references

Examples:

Q1
Q2(a)
Q2(b)
SECTION A
SECTION B

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 5 — ANSWER MATCHING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Match student responses to:

• question numbers
• subparts
• rubric criteria

If uncertain:

State:
"Question match uncertain."

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 6 — STRUCTURED OUTPUT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Generate clean JSON.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

{
  "student_information": {
    "student_name": "",
    "student_id": "",
    "course": "",
    "assignment": ""
  },

  "questions": [
    {
      "question_number": "Q1",
      "page": 2,
      "student_answer": "",
      "diagrams_present": true,
      "tables_present": false,
      "units_present": true,
      "ocr_confidence": "high"
    }
  ]
}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
OCR CONFIDENCE RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Confidence values:

• high
• medium
• low

Use LOW if:

• handwriting unclear
• cropped image
• faded scan
• overlapping writing

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
STAGE 7 — QUALITY CONTROL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Before returning JSON:

✔ Verify all pages processed
✔ Verify all detected questions included
✔ Verify page ordering
✔ Verify OCR completeness
✔ Verify no hallucinated content
✔ Verify JSON validity

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The final output must:

• be structured JSON
• be marking-ready
• preserve all question references
• preserve student wording where possible
• preserve equations and calculations
• contain no invented information

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
IMPORTANT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This stage DOES NOT mark the script.

This stage ONLY:

• validates files
• extracts OCR
• structures answers
• prepares data for the marking agent

title: For the first working version, expect:
content:
[Thu, May 21, 2026 at 01:02 AM]

For the first working version, expect:

Stage	Time
Supabase tables + storage buckets	1–2 hours
Upload page in Bolt.new	2–4 hours
Save files to Supabase	2–4 hours
OCR with Gemini	4–8 hours
Marking JSON output	3–6 hours
Completed rubric display	2–4 hours
PDF export	3–6 hours
Testing/fixing	1–2 days

Realistic total

Basic working prototype:
2–4 days

Reliable version for actual classroom use:
1–2 weeks

Best first target

Aim for:

One student script → completed rubric table → PDF saved in Supabase

Do not add Google Classroom or batch marking until that works.

title: Current Grade: B−V
content:
[Thu, May 21, 2026 at 01:06 I would grade your app as:

Current Grade: B−

Why

Area	Grade	Comment
Core idea	A	Strong, practical, solves a real teaching problem
Workflow design	B+	Good stages: upload → OCR → marking → rubric → PDF
Simplicity	B	Completed rubric report is a smart simplification
Scalability	B−	Needs queue/job handling before many students
Reliability	C+	OCR + AI marking must be carefully controlled
UI/UX	B−	Needs simple teacher flow and mobile-friendly layout
Google Classroom integration	C	Useful, but should come later

Overall Assessment

Your app has strong potential, but the key weakness is reliability.

The app should not try to do everything immediately. It should first prove that it can:

One student script → accurate completed rubric → PDF report

What would move it to A-grade

1. Strict rubric-based marking
2. No hallucinated feedback
3. Completed rubric as the main report
4. Clear per-question marks
5. QC check before PDF
6. One PDF per student
7. Clean teacher dashboard showing status

Final judgement

This is a very good app concept.

At prototype level: B−
With reliable OCR, QC, and PDF export: A−

title: Build a student-specific feedback delivery workflow WITHOUT n8n.  G
content:
[Fri, May 22, 2026 at 09:59 AM]

Build a student-specific feedback delivery workflow WITHOUT n8n.

Goal:
When a full class marking report is generated, the app must automatically split the report into individual student reports, generate one PDF per student, allow teacher preview, then send by email.

Required workflow:
1. The full report must contain clear markers:

=== STUDENT_START ===
Student Name:
Student Email:
Student ID:
Assignment:
Course:
Total Mark:

Completed Rubric:
Corrections:
Teacher Feedback:
=== STUDENT_END ===

2. Save the full report text and PDF to Supabase.

3. Create a Supabase table called student_reports with:

id
assignment_id
course_id
student_name
student_email
student_id
report_text
total_mark
pdf_url
qc_status
send_status
created_at
updated_at

4. Create an Edge Function called split-student-reports.

This function must:
- Read the full report text
- Detect every section between STUDENT_START and STUDENT_END
- Extract student name, email, ID, assignment, course, total mark
- Save each student section into student_reports
- Reject any section missing student name or email
- Return the number of student reports created

5. Create an Edge Function called generate-student-pdf.

This function must:
- Take one student_report record
- Convert report_text into a clean PDF
- Save the PDF in Supabase Storage
- Update pdf_url in student_reports

6. Add a teacher preview page called Student Feedback Reports.

The page must show a table with:
- Student Name
- Email
- Total Mark
- QC Status
- Send Status
- Preview PDF button
- Send Email button

7. Add a QC check before sending.

The QC check must verify:
- The student name is present
- The email is present
- The PDF exists
- The total mark is present
- The section does not contain another student's email
- The section does not contain another student's name

If QC fails, block sending and show the error.

8. Create an Edge Function called send-student-email.

This function must:
- Get the selected student report
- Attach the PDF
- Send the email using the app email provider
- Update send_status to emailed
- Log the sent date and time

Email subject:
Marked Feedback Report - [Assignment Name]

Email body:
Dear [Student Name],

Please find attached your marked feedback report for [Assignment Name].

Review the completed rubric, corrections, and teacher feedback carefully.

Regards,
Mr Reid

9. Add status badges:
- draft
- pdf_generated
- qc_passed
- qc_failed
- emailed
- failed

10. Important privacy rule:
Never send the full class report to a student.
Only send the PDF generated from that student's own extracted section.

11. Add a batch button:
Generate All Student PDFs

12. Add another batch button:
Send All QC-Passed Reports

Before batch sending, show a confirmation summary:
- Number of reports ready
- Number blocked by QC
- Number missing emails
- Number already sent

Do not send anything without teacher confirmation.

Build this cleanly into the existing assignment/report workflow.

title: Add a professional multi-export system to my app using the existing file tick-box selection system.
content:
[Sat, May 23, 2026 at 08:41 AM]

Add a professional multi-export system to my app using the existing file tick-box selection system.

The app already has:
- file list
- file preview
- file select tick box
- single-file download
- storage system

Do NOT rebuild the file selection system.

Use the already selected/ticked files and create a bulk export workflow.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MAIN REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

When one or more files are selected, display a bulk action toolbar.

The toolbar must show:
- selected file count
- export buttons
- clear selection button

Toolbar buttons:

[ZIP]
[Combined PDF]
[Email]
[Google Drive]
[CSV/XLSX]
[Classroom]
[Share Link]
[Clear]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. ZIP EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

When user clicks ZIP:

- gather all selected files
- send selected file metadata to backend
- backend downloads files from storage
- backend creates ZIP archive
- browser downloads ZIP automatically

ZIP filename format:
selected-files-yyyy-mm-dd.zip

Show progress states:
- Preparing ZIP...
- Compressing files...
- Downloading ZIP...
- ZIP ready.

Handle errors:
- missing file
- corrupted file
- ZIP creation failed

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. COMBINED PDF EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add Combined PDF export.

Behaviour:
- merge selected PDFs into one professional PDF
- preserve file order
- optionally insert separator pages with student names or document titles

Combined PDF filename:
combined-report-yyyy-mm-dd.pdf

Useful for:
- moderation
- grading review
- department submissions
- archiving

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. EMAIL EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

When user clicks Email:

Open modal with:
- recipient email
- optional CC
- optional subject
- optional message

Backend workflow:
- create ZIP
- if ZIP < 20MB:
    attach ZIP directly
- if ZIP > 20MB:
    upload ZIP to storage
    generate secure download link
    email link instead

Add status messages:
- Preparing email...
- Sending files...
- Email sent successfully.
- Email failed.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. GOOGLE DRIVE EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add Google Drive export.

Workflow:
- authenticate user with Google
- upload selected files or ZIP to Drive
- create folders automatically if needed

Recommended folder structure:

Course/
   Week/
      Reports/
      Scripts/
      Rubrics/

Show:
- Uploading to Drive...
- Upload complete.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. CSV/XLSX GRADEBOOK EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add export to:
- CSV
- Excel XLSX

Export fields:
- student name
- ID
- assignment
- marks
- percentage
- grade
- comments

Filename examples:
ENGR405_Gradebook_Week5.csv
ENGR405_Gradebook_Week5.xlsx

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. GOOGLE CLASSROOM EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add Classroom integration.

Allow:
- return rubric PDF
- return marked script
- attach feedback files
- post grades

Add button:
[Return to Classroom]

Support:
- selected students
- selected assignments
- bulk return

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. SECURE SHARE LINK EXPORT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add:
[Generate Share Link]

Workflow:
- upload files or ZIP to storage
- generate secure signed URL

Options:
- expiry date
- password protection
- view-only
- download-only

Display:
- copy link button
- QR code optional

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

On desktop:
- toolbar appears horizontally

On mobile:
- toolbar scrolls horizontally
OR collapses into dropdown menu

Ensure:
- large tap targets
- no overflow issues
- responsive spacing

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. UI/UX REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Use:
- modern floating toolbar
- subtle animations
- loading indicators
- success/error toasts
- progress bars

Selected files should remain highlighted.

Toolbar should remain visible while files are selected.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. ARCHITECTURE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement using:
- reusable export service layer
- modular API routes
- clean async handling
- proper error boundaries

Do not break:
- existing file preview
- current uploads
- single-file download
- delete features
- current storage system

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11. FUTURE EXPANSION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Structure the export system so future exports can easily be added:
- OneDrive
- Dropbox
- Moodle
- Canvas LMS
- WhatsApp export
- Telegram export
- Overleaf export

Create the implementation cleanly and professionally.

title: Recommended Structure
content:
[Sun, May 24, 2026 at 08:29 AM]

Yes — better structure:

Recommended Structure

Assignment To-Do Card
 ├── Rubric
 ├── Question Paper
 ├── Student Scripts
 │    ├── John Brown.pdf
 │    ├── Sarah Smith.pdf
 │    └── Mark Lee.pdf
 ├── Mark All Scripts
 ├── Progress Tracker
 └── Export Reports

So instead of one card per student, you create one card per assignment, and inside it you store all student scripts.

Card Fields

Field	Example
assignment_title	MATH095 Week 3 Assignment
course	MATH095
question_paper_url	PDF
rubric_url	PDF
scripts_count	42
status	Ready for Marking
marking_progress	18/42 marked

Each Script Inside the Card

Student Script Row
 ├── Student Name
 ├── File
 ├── OCR Status
 ├── Marks
 ├── QC Status
 ├── Report PDF
 └── Email / Classroom Return

Best Workflow

Upload ZIP
 ↓
Extract scripts
 ↓
Create ONE assignment card
 ↓
Attach all scripts inside that card
 ↓
Mark scripts individually or bulk mark all

Bolt.new Prompt

Update the to-do list system so that each to-do card represents ONE assignment, not one student.
Inside each assignment to-do card, allow multiple student scripts to be attached, listed, processed, marked, and exported.
Requirements:
1. Add support for uploading a ZIP file of student scripts into one assignment to-do card.
2. When a ZIP is uploaded:
   - extract all valid files
   - preserve student names where possible from folder names or filenames
   - upload each extracted script to Supabase Storage
   - create one student-script record per file
   - attach all script records to the same assignment to-do card
3. The assignment to-do card should display:
   - assignment title
   - course
   - question paper
   - rubric
   - number of scripts uploaded
   - marking progress
   - overall status
4. Inside the card, show a Student Scripts section with rows:
   - student name
   - filename
   - file type
   - OCR status
   - marking status
   - QC status
   - marks awarded
   - report PDF link
   - actions: View, Mark, QC, Generate Report, Email, Send to Classroom
5. Add buttons:
   - Upload ZIP
   - Add More Scripts
   - Mark All Scripts
   - Run QC for All
   - Generate All Reports
   - Download All Reports as ZIP
   - Email Reports to Students
   - Return Reports to Google Classroom
6. Do not create a separate to-do card for every student.
   Create only one assignment card, then attach all scripts as child records.
7. Add duplicate detection:
   - do not upload the same student script twice
   - if duplicate is found, ask whether to replace, skip, or save as new version
8. Add progress tracking:
   - Uploaded: 42
   - OCR completed: 40
   - Marked: 38
   - QC passed: 36
   - Reports generated: 36
9. Use this relationship:
assignment_todo_cards
   has many student_scripts
student_scripts
   belongs to assignment_todo_cards
10. Keep the UI mobile friendly:
   - show summary on the card
   - expand/collapse student script list
   - allow search by student name
   - allow filter by status

This is the cleaner design for your app.Analyse with Gemini

 │    ├── John Brown.pdf
 │    ├── Sarah Smith.pdf
 │    └── Mark Lee.pdf
 ├── Mark All Scripts
 ├── Progress Tracker
 └── Export Reports

So instead of one card per student, you create one card per assignment, and inside it you store all student scripts.

Card Fields

Field	Example
assignment_title	MATH095 Week 3 Assignment
course	MATH095
question_paper_url	PDF
rubric_url	PDF
scripts_count	42
status	Ready for Marking
marking_progress	18/42 marked

Each Script Inside the Card

Student Script Row
 ├── Student Name
 ├── File
 ├── OCR Status
 ├── Marks
 ├── QC Status
 ├── Report PDF
 └── Email / Classroom Return

Best Workflow

Upload ZIP
 ↓
Extract scripts
 ↓
Create ONE assignment card
 ↓
Attach all scripts inside that card
 ↓
Mark scripts individually or bulk mark all

Bolt.new Prompt

Update the to-do list system so that each to-do card represents ONE assignment, not one student.
Inside each assignment to-do card, allow multiple student scripts to be attached, listed, processed, marked, and exported.
Requirements:
1. Add support for uploading a ZIP file of student scripts into one assignment to-do card.
2. When a ZIP is uploaded:
   - extract all valid files
   - preserve student names where possible from folder names or filenames
   - upload each extracted script to Supabase Storage
   - create one student-script record per file
   - attach all script records to the same assignment to-do card
3. The assignment to-do card should display:
   - assignment title
   - course
   - question paper
   - rubric
   - number of scripts uploaded
   - marking progress
   - overall status
4. Inside the card, show a Student Scripts section with rows:
   - student name
   - filename
   - file type
   - OCR status
   - marking status
   - QC status
   - marks awarded
   - report PDF link
   - actions: View, Mark, QC, Generate Report, Email, Send to Classroom
5. Add buttons:
   - Upload ZIP
   - Add More Scripts
   - Mark All Scripts
   - Run QC for All
   - Generate All Reports
   - Download All Reports as ZIP
   - Email Reports to Students
   - Return Reports to Google Classroom
6. Do not create a separate to-do card for every student.
   Create only one assignment card, then attach all scripts as child records.
7. Add duplicate detection:
   - do not upload the same student script twice
   - if duplicate is found, ask whether to replace, skip, or save as new version
8. Add progress tracking:
   - Uploaded: 42
   - OCR completed: 40
   - Marked: 38
   - QC passed: 36
   - Reports generated: 36
9. Use this relationship:
assignment_todo_cards
   has many student_scripts
student_scripts
   belongs to assignment_todo_cards
10. Keep the UI mobile friendly:
   - show summary on the card
   - expand/collapse student script list
   - allow search by student name
   - allow filter by status

This is the cleaner design for your app.

title: Mark Script with AI and General Analysis
content:
[Sun, May 24, 2026, 03:48 AM]
0123456789
 files read
Now I have a complete understanding. Let me give a clear explanation:

Mark Script with AI and General Analysis are two distinct tools with very different purposes:

Mark Script with AI	General Analysis
Edge function	mark-student-script	analyze-attachments
Input	One student PDF + optional question paper	Any number of files (PDFs, images, etc.)
What it does	Structured marking — question by question, assigns marks, compares to a rubric, produces a scored annotated LaTeX report	Open-ended analysis — summarises, identifies themes, compares students, or does whatever the prompt says
Output	Structured JSON (sections, per-question scores, feedback, total score) → compiled into a per-student feedback PDF with a cover page showing the grade	Markdown/text pasted into the note, optionally with a class summary table
Response format	Server-Sent Events (streaming)	Single JSON response
Multiple scripts	Yes — iterates through each non-QP PDF one at a time, calling the edge function separately for each	Yes — sends all selected files in one batch call
Saves a PDF	Yes — each student gets their own AI-marked report PDF attached to the card	Saves the analysis as a PDF attachment
Grade tracking	Updates grade_percentage on the attachment	Updates grade_percentage only if the AI returns scores
Rubric	Pulls the lesson's rubric automatically	Optionally uses rubric if one is set
Question paper	Detected automatically from filename (QP/assignment keywords) and sent alongside the script	Not applicable
In short: "Mark Script with AI" is a formal marking tool — it marks one script at a time with per-question feedback and a grade. "General Analysis" is a flexible AI prompt tool — it sends all selected files to the AI with a custom prompt and returns free-form analysis, useful for batch summaries, cross-student comparisons, or any non-structured task.

content:
[Sun, May 24, 2026 at 01:56 PM]

Here is a professional Bolt.new prompt for implementing automatic manual-grade saving into your AI Reports component with Supabase integration, audit tracking, QC compatibility, and lecturer override support.

You are improving the AI Reports component of an academic marking and QC platform built with Bolt.new, React, TypeScript, and Supabase.

GOAL:
When a lecturer manually edits or overrides a grade inside the AI Reports component, the updated grade must automatically save to the Supabase database and instantly update the UI without requiring a page refresh.

IMPLEMENT THE FOLLOWING FEATURES:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. DATABASE UPDATE FLOW
    ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

When a grade field is edited:

• Detect the change
• Save automatically on:

* blur
* Enter key
* or 1-second debounce after typing

Use Supabase update queries.

Example target table:

student_reports

Fields:

* id
* student_name
* ai_grade
* manual_grade
* final_grade
* qc_status
* updated_by
* updated_at

Rules:

* NEVER overwrite ai_grade
* manual_grade stores lecturer edits
* final_grade becomes:
    manual_grade if present
    otherwise ai_grade

Update updated_at automatically.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. UI/UX REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Inside the AI Report card display:

AI Grade: 67%
Manual Override: [ editable input ]
Final Grade: 72%

Requirements:

* clean professional academic layout
* mobile responsive
* works on iPhone, Fold, tablet, and desktop
* instant visual update after save
* loading spinner while saving
* success toast:
    “Grade saved”
* error toast:
    “Save failed”

Add:

* “Modified by Lecturer”
* timestamp of last edit

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. AUTO-SAVE LOGIC
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement:

* optimistic UI updates
* debounce saving
* prevent duplicate saves
* retry failed saves once
* disable save loop triggering

Use:

* React hooks
* async/await
* TypeScript
* Supabase client

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. AUDIT TRAIL SYSTEM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Create a new table:

grade_history

Fields:

* id
* report_id
* old_grade
* new_grade
* changed_by
* changed_at
* reason

Every manual edit must:

* insert a history record
* preserve previous value
* timestamp the change

This is required for:

* moderation
* accreditation
* QC auditing
* disputes

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. QC INTEGRATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If a manual grade differs from the AI grade:

Automatically:

* set qc_status = “modified”
* display warning badge:
    “Lecturer Override”
* preserve original AI output

Add a confidence indicator:

AI Confidence:

* High
* Medium
* Low

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. PROFESSIONAL FEATURES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add:

* revert to AI grade button
* lock final grades option
* publish/unpublish toggle
* section-level override support
* comments field for lecturer justification

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The solution must:

* feel instant
* avoid full page reloads
* use local state efficiently
* batch updates where possible
* scale to hundreds of reports

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. CODE QUALITY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Generate:

* clean modular React components
* reusable hooks
* TypeScript interfaces
* Supabase service helpers
* proper error handling
* loading states
* comments explaining logic

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. MOBILE EXPERIENCE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Ensure:

* inputs resize properly
* touch-friendly controls
* no horizontal overflow
* responsive report cards
* smooth scrolling on mobile

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. OUTPUT REQUIREMENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Generate:

* complete implementation
* updated component code
* Supabase queries
* SQL schema updates
* hooks
* helper functions
* responsive styling
* production-ready code

The implementation must integrate cleanly with the existing AI marking, QC, and rubric workflow system.

Fix the AI Reports component so it properly handles reports with no question-by-question breakdown.
Current message shown:
“No question-by-question breakdown available.
This report was generated before per-question tracking was added...”
Required behaviour:
1. If a report has no per-question breakdown:
   - Show a clear warning panel.
   - Do NOT treat it as an error.
   - Explain that the report is an older format.
   - Provide an action button: “Re-run AI Marking”.
2. When the teacher clicks “Re-run AI Marking”:
   - Open the Analyse workflow for the original student script.
   - Automatically load:
     - assessment paper
     - rubric
     - student script
     - course/assignment metadata
   - Run the full 5-stage marking pipeline.
   - Save the new question-by-question breakdown to the database.
3. Database requirements:
   - Preserve the old report.
   - Add/replace the per-question breakdown in the new report record.
   - Save:
     - question number
     - max marks
     - awarded marks
     - student answer evidence
     - rubric criterion
     - feedback
     - section total
     - final total
4. UI requirements:
   - Display a loading state while re-marking.
   - Show pipeline stages:
     Stage 1: Preparing Documents
     Stage 2: Extracting Evidence
     Stage 3: Marking Script
     Stage 4: Quality Control
     Stage 5: Report Generation
   - Show success message:
     “Question-by-question breakdown generated successfully.”
   - Refresh the report automatically after completion.
5. Safety rule:
   - Do not invent missing question scores.
   - Only generate the breakdown by re-running the marking pipeline on the original uploaded student script and rubric.
Make the implementation clean, TypeScript-safe, mobile responsive, and fully integrated with Supabase.

title: AI Reports grading system
content:
[Sun, May 24, 2026 at 04:23 PM]

You are improving the AI Reports grading system for an educational assessment platform that uses Supabase as the backend database.

GOAL:
Ensure that every AI-generated or manually edited grade inside the AI Reports component is reliably saved to the database, verified after saving, and visibly confirmed to the user.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUIRED FEATURES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. DATABASE SAVE VERIFICATION

Whenever a grade is:
- AI generated
- manually edited
- recalculated
- imported
- QC adjusted

the system MUST:

A. Save the updated report to Supabase
B. Verify the save succeeded
C. Update the UI only AFTER successful save
D. Show visible save status

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. SAVE STATUS INDICATORS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add persistent save indicators to every report:

🟡 Saving...
🟢 Saved to Database
🔴 Save Failed
⚪ Unsaved Changes

Display:
- timestamp
- report ID
- last modified time
- sync status

Example:

Grade Saved Successfully
Student: John Brown
Score: 24/30
Saved: 14:22:11
Report ID: rpt_82hf22

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. SUPABASE SAVE LOGIC
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement proper Supabase persistence.

DO NOT only update React local state.

BAD:
setReports(updatedReports)

GOOD:
await supabase
  .from('ai_reports')
  .update({
    total_marks,
    percentage,
    question_breakdown,
    feedback,
    updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
  })
  .eq('id', reportId)

After save:
- re-fetch the saved row
- verify values match
- only then update UI state

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. AUTO-REFRESH VALIDATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

After save:
- automatically refresh report data from Supabase
- confirm displayed values equal database values

If mismatch occurs:
- show warning
- retry save
- log error

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. UNSAVED CHANGES DETECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Track dirty state.

If user edits a grade but it is not saved:
- show Unsaved Changes badge
- prevent accidental navigation loss
- show confirmation dialog before leaving page

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. GRADE AUDIT LOG
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Create a database audit table:

grade_audit_log

Fields:
- id
- report_id
- student_name
- modified_by
- action_type
- old_grade
- new_grade
- timestamp
- notes

Every manual edit or AI overwrite MUST create an audit entry.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. SAVE FAILURE HANDLING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If Supabase fails:
- keep local edits temporarily
- show retry button
- never silently discard grades

Display detailed error messages:
- permission denied
- network timeout
- row missing
- RLS blocked
- invalid payload

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. MOBILE-FIRST SAVE UX
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Ensure save indicators work properly on:
- iPhone
- Android
- iPad
- Foldable devices
- desktop

Status badges must remain visible even while scrolling long reports.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. REPORT METADATA
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Each AI report record should contain:

{
  student_name,
  student_id,
  course_code,
  assessment_name,
  total_marks,
  percentage,
  question_breakdown,
  rubric_used,
  qc_status,
  saved_by,
  created_at,
  updated_at,
  sync_status
}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. REPORT SAVE PIPELINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement this exact workflow:

Edit Grade
    ↓
Validate Data
    ↓
Save to Supabase
    ↓
Verify Database Write
    ↓
Refresh Saved Record
    ↓
Update Local UI State
    ↓
Show Saved Badge
    ↓
Write Audit Log

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11. DEBUG PANEL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add optional developer debug panel showing:
- database connection status
- last save payload
- Supabase response
- save latency
- failed requests
- retry count

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
12. DATABASE SAFETY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Prevent:
- duplicate saves
- race conditions
- partial updates
- overwriting newer edits
- accidental grade resets

Use:
- optimistic locking OR
- updated_at version checks

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13. REQUIRED UI IMPROVEMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add:
- floating save indicator
- auto-save every 15 seconds
- manual save button
- retry failed save button
- “last saved” timestamp
- save history viewer

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
14. TESTING REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Create test cases for:
- successful save
- failed save
- offline mode
- duplicate edits
- simultaneous edits
- refresh persistence
- mobile usage
- large reports
- QC modifications

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
15. FINAL REQUIREMENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system MUST guarantee:

If the user refreshes the page/app after editing grades,
the grades remain exactly as saved.

No grade should ever appear saved visually unless it is confirmed stored in Supabase.

8. MOBILE-FIRST SAVE UX
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Ensure save indicators work properly on:
- iPhone
- Android
- iPad
- Foldable devices
- desktop

Status badges must remain visible even while scrolling long reports.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. REPORT METADATA
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Each AI report record should contain:

{
  student_name,
  student_id,
  course_code,
  assessment_name,
  total_marks,
  percentage,
  question_breakdown,
  rubric_used,
  qc_status,
  saved_by,
  created_at,
  updated_at,
  sync_status
}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. REPORT SAVE PIPELINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement this exact workflow:

Edit Grade
    ↓
Validate Data
    ↓
Save to Supabase
    ↓
Verify Database Write
    ↓
Refresh Saved Record
    ↓
Update Local UI State
    ↓
Show Saved Badge
    ↓
Write Audit Log

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11. DEBUG PANEL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add optional developer debug panel showing:
- database connection status
- last save payload
- Supabase response
- save latency
- failed requests
- retry count

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
12. DATABASE SAFETY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Prevent:
- duplicate saves
- race conditions
- partial updates
- overwriting newer edits
- accidental grade resets

Use:
- optimistic locking OR
- updated_at version checks

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13. REQUIRED UI IMPROVEMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Add:
- floating save indicator
- auto-save every 15 seconds
- manual save button
- retry failed save button
- “last saved” timestamp
- save history viewer

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
14. TESTING REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Create test cases for:
- successful save
- failed save
- offline mode
- duplicate edits
- simultaneous edits
- refresh persistence
- mobile usage
- large reports
- QC modifications

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
15. FINAL REQUIREMENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The system MUST guarantee:

If the user refreshes the page/app after editing grades,
the grades remain exactly as saved.

No grade should ever appear saved visually unless it is confirmed stored in Supabase.
